commit.message:
feat: Add Maya's centralized persona with research-backed SDR temperament

Create persona.py with Maya's full personality definition (backstory,
temperament, values, communication style) grounded in SDR psychology
research. Wire it into all 4 Claude API calls via system= parameter.
Strip scattered inline personality rules from user prompts.

Maya's temperament: calm, grounded, slightly outgoing. Genuine but
restrained warmth. Substance over energy. Not performatively friendly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Shrink 2-layer PCB from 100x80mm to 90x70mm

- Improve layout algorithm for small boards:
  resistor stacking near DIN-6, cap collision avoidance,
  adaptive test point spacing
- Board area reduced 21% (8000mm² → 6300mm²)
- DRC clean: 0 critical, 0 warnings, 0 unconnected

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(data): corriger coordonnées GPS du Lycée Privé Sainte Maure (Aube)

Les coordonnées pointaient vers les Ardennes (49.868, 4.430).
Correction vers Sainte-Maure, Aube, près de Troyes (48.346, 4.058).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix event expiry logic to consider starts_at before falling back to created_at

Events with no ends_at were expiring based on created_at + 72h, causing
future events (e.g. March 28) to show as expired days before they happen.
Now uses COALESCE(ends_at, starts_at, created_at) consistently across
frontend, edge function, and database.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: extract tmux.conf inline scripts into standalone files

Move the three long one-liner shell commands (queue status bar, queue
fzf popup, queue pause) into readable scripts under configs/tmux/.
Update help text labels to match (Prompt:N, Idle:N, Thinking:N, Paused:N).
Also let tminit reload config even when run outside a tmux session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  con hashes que rompe el empaquetado en macOS)
- schema.prisma: agrega binaryTargets darwin y darwin-arm64 para que
  Prisma incluya binarios de Mac (Intel y Apple Silicon)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: link C++ runtime for Tesseract static linking on all targets

Tesseract is a C++ library with a C API (capi.h). When FFmpeg's
configure tests linking via the C compiler, C++ symbols from
libtesseract.a are unresolved unless the C++ runtime is explicitly
linked. Add -lstdc++ (Linux/mingw) or -lc++ (macOS/llvm-mingw)
to extra-ldflags on all platforms.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Release: merge develop into main (#166)

* Add draft WG implementation

* Add lifecycle management for sandbox processes

Introduces new lifecycle features to the sandbox API, including the ability to force stop processes and retrieve the current sandbox status. The `/stop` endpoint allows for immediate or scheduled removal of the keepAlive flag from running processes, enabling auto-hibernation. The `/status` endpoint provides information on the current state of the sandbox and active keepAlive processes.

Additionally, integrates the lifecycle management with the MCP tools, enhancing the overall process control and monitoring capabilities. The scale-to-zero functionality is also improved with crash recovery mechanisms.

Updates include:
- New `LifecycleHandler` for managing lifecycle operations.
- API documentation updates for new endpoints.
- Integration of keepAlive functionality in process management.
- Comprehensive tests for lifecycle features and MCP integration.

* Enhance scale-to-zero functionality with improved error handling and logging

* Remove lifecycle management endpoints and related functionality

This commit removes the `/stop` and `/status` endpoints from the sandbox API, along with the associated `LifecycleHandler` and related data structures. The lifecycle management features, including the ability to force stop processes and retrieve the current sandbox status, have been deprecated.

Updates include:
- Deletion of lifecycle-related API routes and handlers.
- Removal of lifecycle management types and structures from the codebase.
- Adjustments to documentation to reflect the removal of these features.

This change simplifies the API and focuses on core process management functionalities.

* Fix PR

* Fix bug introduce with AI check

* Implement keepAlive timeout handling for restarted processes

This commit introduces functionality to manage timeouts for processes with the keepAlive flag enabled. If a process is restarted and keepAlive is active with a specified timeout, a goroutine is initiated to monitor the timeout and kill the process if it exceeds the limit. For processes with an infinite timeout, the goroutine simply waits for the process to complete. This enhancement improves process management and ensures better resource handling.

* Add terminal session to web terminal

Add better session handling

* Force rebuild

* Implement network tunnel management endpoints and graceful shutdown for WireGuard client

- Added endpoints for managing network tunnels:
  - `DELETE /network/tunnel` to disconnect the tunnel and restore the original network configuration.
  - `PUT /network/tunnel/config` to apply a new tunnel configuration on the fly.

- Introduced graceful shutdown handling in the main server to ensure proper cleanup of resources when receiving termination signals.
- Updated OpenAPI documentation to reflect the new endpoints and their functionalities.
- Added tests for the new networking features to ensure reliability and correctness.

* Refactor WireGuard route management to replace default route handling

- Updated the setupRoutes method to delete the existing default route via the physical interface and add a new default route through the WireGuard interface.
- Modified the removeRoutes method to restore the original default route after removing the WireGuard default route.
- Improved logging for route addition and deletion to enhance visibility during network configuration changes.

* Refactor WireGuard route handling to use parsed CIDR for default routes

- Updated the setupRoutes and removeRoutes methods to replace nil default route handling with parsed CIDR for improved clarity and maintainability.
- Ensured consistent handling of default routes across both methods to enhance code readability.

* Use batch instead of lambda to build images

* Add docker template

* Fix docker

* Fix no slim check

* Add docker in choice of template

* Allow to mount/umount agentdrive from endpoint

* Allow to mount/umount agentdrive from endpoint (#147)

* Fix mount

* Fix env var

* Fix list and umount

* Fix list mount

* Change routing for drives attachment

* Update sandbox template name

* Add Linux-specific route monitoring and error handling for WireGuard

- Introduced a new file for Linux-specific route monitoring, which handles route changes and removes conflicting default routes.
- Added error handling for unsupported operations on non-Linux platforms in the wireguard_other.go file.
- Updated the WireGuardClient struct to include a channel for stopping the route monitor.
- Enhanced the WireGuard client methods to manage route monitoring and ensure proper cleanup during shutdown.

* Update endpoint for mount drive

* Update endpoint for mount drive (#150)

* Fix prefix for agt drive

* Cdrappier/agent drive (#151)

* Update endpoint for mount drive

* Fix prefix for agt drive

* Enhance drive management with validation and improved routing

- Added validation for drive names and mount paths to prevent security issues such as path traversal and injection.
- Updated DELETE endpoint for drives to streamline the routing logic.
- Introduced unit tests for validation functions to ensure robustness.
- Improved error handling in drive mounting and unmounting processes.

* Refine drive routing and enhance mount path normalization

- Updated DELETE endpoint for drives to include the correct path prefix.
- Introduced normalization for mount paths to ensure they start with a '/'.
- Enhanced validation tests for mount paths to cover various scenarios.
- Removed unnecessary constants related to allowed mount base.

* Remove dead code

* Fix path token

* fix: prevent log duplication in stream by only re-reading log file when no data was streamed

* Improve CI speed by making arm64 image not mandatory to continue

* Fix log tail race: drain all output before signaling done and reset channels on restart

* feat: add browser-based sandbox hub templates (ENG-1991)

Add 5 new sandbox templates ported from blaxel-ai/uvm:
- playwright-firefox: Playwright server with Firefox browser
- playwright-chromium: Playwright server with Chromium browser
- chromium: Headless Chromium with CDP + nginx proxy + sandbox-api
- cua-xfce: CUA XFCE desktop with VNC/noVNC + computer-server + sandbox-api
- xfce-vnc: XFCE desktop with VNC/noVNC + sandbox-api (no CUA computer-server)

Also updates CI workflow_dispatch options to include the new templates.

Co-Authored-By: cploujoux <cploujoux@blaxel.ai>

* fix: address review issues - icon URL, EXPOSE 8081, xfce-vnc supervisor config (ENG-1991)

- Fix broken chromium icon URL
- Add EXPOSE 8081 to playwright Dockerfiles for playwright server port
- Replace xfce-vnc supervisor config properly to remove computer-server
  (overrides the single supervisord.conf instead of trying to rm a non-existent file)

Co-Authored-By: cploujoux <cploujoux@blaxel.ai>

* fix: address remaining review comments (ENG-1991)

- Replace wait -n with POSIX-compatible wait in chromium entrypoint (busybox ash compatibility)
- Swap playwright foreground/background: sandbox-api runs in background, playwright in foreground via exec (crash detection)
- Change nginx error_log from debug to warn level

Co-Authored-By: cploujoux <cploujoux@blaxel.ai>

* fix: use --js-flags for V8 max-old-space-size in chromium entrypoint (ENG-1991)

--max_old_space_size is a V8/Node.js flag, not a Chromium browser switch.
Chromium silently ignores it. The correct way to pass V8 flags is via --js-flags.

Co-Authored-By: cploujoux <cploujoux@blaxel.ai>

* Fix xfce-vnc supervisord.conf path to replace main config instead of adding a conf.d snippet

* Update hub/xfce-vnc/supervisord.conf

Co-authored-by: devin-ai-integration[bot] <158243242+devin-ai-integration[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: update icon URLs for cua, xfce-vnc, and chromium templates (ENG-1991)

Co-Authored-By: cploujoux <cploujoux@blaxel.ai>

* fix: use trap-based exit on first process failure in chromium entrypoint (ENG-1991)

Replace plain 'wait' with a poll loop + trap so the container exits
immediately when any of the three background processes (chrome, sandbox-api,
nginx) dies, and cleans up the remaining processes via the EXIT trap.

Co-Authored-By: cploujoux <cploujoux@blaxel.ai>

* fix: add trap-based process monitoring to playwright entrypoints (ENG-1991)

Both playwright-chromium and playwright-firefox entrypoints now use the same
trap-based poll loop as chromium, so the container exits immediately when
either sandbox-api or playwright dies and cleans up the remaining process.

Co-Authored-By: cploujoux <cploujoux@blaxel.ai>

* Simplify hub categories: consolidate 18 categories down to 6

Consolidated categories from 18 granular ones (react, nodejs, typescript,
Python, docker, virtualization, minimal, optimization, claude, openai,
google, xai, code, etc.) down to 6 clear user-facing categories:

- web: Next.js, Vite React TS
- mobile: Expo
- backend: Node, Python App, TypeScript App
- devops: Docker in Sandbox, Benchmark
- data-science: Jupyter Notebook, Jupyter Server
- ai: All VibeKit templates (Claude, Codex, Gemini, Grok, OpenCode)

Co-Authored-By: cploujoux <cploujoux@blaxel.ai>

* Remove categories for vibekit hub

* Add Astro template and start dev servers at startup for Next.js and Vite

- Add new Astro hub template with Dockerfile, entrypoint, astro.config.mjs, and template.json
- Update Next.js: add entrypoint.sh to start dev server via sandbox-api process endpoint at startup, add next.config.ts with allowedDevOrigins
- Update Vite: add entrypoint.sh to start dev server via sandbox-api process endpoint at startup
- All three templates now follow the coding-agent pattern: sandbox-api starts first, then the dev server is launched via the process API with restartOnFailure

Co-Authored-By: cploujoux <cploujoux@blaxel.ai>

* Fix Astro config: move allowedHosts to vite.server block

allowedHosts is a Vite-specific option, not an Astro server option.
Moved it to the vite.server config block where it will be properly applied.

Co-Authored-By: cploujoux <cploujoux@blaxel.ai>

* Add Astro to CI workflow and fix Vite port config

- Add 'astro' option to workflow_dispatch in build.yaml (alphabetized list)
- Add explicit port 5173 to vite.config.ts
- Add explicit --port 5173 to Vite entrypoint command

Co-Authored-By: cploujoux <cploujoux@blaxel.ai>

* Move Next.js host/port config to env vars instead of CLI flags

Use PORT=3000 HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0 env vars which Next.js natively respects,
instead of passing --port 3000 as a CLI flag.

Co-Authored-By: cploujoux <cploujoux@blaxel.ai>

* Update entrypoint

* Rename categories

* Fix astro config

* Adapt Expo template based on bl-test pattern

- Install curl and netcat-openbsd for entrypoint health checks
- Install web deps (react-dom, react-native-web, expo-asset)
- Patch expo-asset for HTTPS proxy support (http->https rewrite)
- Pre-warm Metro bundler cache during build
- Add entrypoint.sh to auto-start Expo dev server via sandbox-api
- Bump memory to 8192 (matches bl-test config)
- Simplify ports to sandbox-api (8080) + expo-web (8081)

Co-Authored-By: cploujoux <cploujoux@blaxel.ai>

* feat: add sample test scripts to playwright sandbox templates (ENG-1991)

Add sample-test.js to both playwright-chromium and playwright-firefox
templates. Each script connects to the local Playwright server and
verifies browser functionality by loading example.com and Wikipedia.

Co-Authored-By: cploujoux <cploujoux@blaxel.ai>

* fix: replace console.assert with node assert module for proper error handling (ENG-1991)

console.assert in Node.js only logs to stderr without throwing or exiting
non-zero. Switch to the built-in assert module so failures actually halt
execution and produce a non-zero exit code.

Co-Authored-By: cploujoux <cploujoux@blaxel.ai>

* feat: add lightpanda browser sandbox hub template (ENG-1991)

Add lightweight LightPanda browser template with CDP support. Includes:
- Dockerfile based on lightpanda/browser:latest with sandbox-api
- Trap-based process monitoring entrypoint
- template.json with 1024MB memory (lightweight vs 8GB for full browsers)
- Sample Puppeteer test script for verification
- CI workflow_dispatch option

Co-Authored-By: cploujoux <cploujoux@blaxel.ai>

* fix: remove sample-test.js from Docker image — no Node.js in lightpanda base (ENG-1991)

The lightpanda/browser:latest image is minimal (~60MB) with no Node.js
runtime. Keep the sample test as a repo-level reference only, not copied
into the container.

Co-Authored-By: cploujoux <cploujoux@blaxel.ai>

* Fix copy for lightpanda

* fix: reduce memory limits and update lightpanda metadata (ENG-1991)

Adjust default memory allocation from 8GB to 4GB for resource-intensive templates (XFCE, Playwright, and Docker). Also update Lightpanda's display name casing and icon URL for consistency.

* fix: update Playwright and Expo template configurations (ENG-1991)

Upgrade Playwright to 1.58.2 and ensure local installation for script compatibility. For Expo, remove the --no-dev-client flag and reduce the Metro bundler cache pre-warm timeout.

* feat: export process logs to stdout with structured attributes (ENG-1998)

Add logrus logging in readAndBroadcast to print process stdout/stderr
to the sandbox-api's own stdout. Uses structured log fields (source,
processName, processPid, stream) so the telemetry collector can
distinguish process logs from access logs.

- stdout lines logged at INFO level
- stderr lines logged at ERROR level
- Each line logged separately for clean telemetry ingestion

Co-Authored-By: cploujoux <cploujoux@blaxel.ai>

* fix: use TrimSuffix instead of TrimRight for semantic correctness

Co-Authored-By: cploujoux <cploujoux@blaxel.ai>

* feat(ENG-2014): implement audit logging for sandbox access with user ID and request ID

- Add audit logging package (src/lib/audit) with blaxel_source=audit attribute
- Add IdentityMiddleware to extract X-Blaxel-Subject-Id, X-Blaxel-Subject-Type,
  X-Blaxel-Auth-Method, and X-Request-Id (or generated UUID) from headers
- Audit terminal connections (connect/disconnect) with session context
- Audit process execution, log access, stop, and kill operations
- Add comprehensive tests for the audit package
- Audit logs are separate from process logs (source=process) and access logs

Co-Authored-By: cploujoux <cploujoux@blaxel.ai>

* fix: restore logrus output to os.Stderr in test teardown

Address Mendral review comment - logrus.SetOutput(nil) can cause
nil-pointer panic on subsequent log calls.

Co-Authored-By: cploujoux <cploujoux@blaxel.ai>

* fix: use 'source' field name to match existing OTel attribute mapping convention

Co-Authored-By: cploujoux <cploujoux@blaxel.ai>

* Fix alignment

* Add access  souce for access log

* Add terminal command audit logging with PTY input reconstruction

* fix(ENG-2014): fix OSC escape sequence parser and add terminal command buffer tests

Replace the single inEscape bool with a 5-state machine (None/Start/CSI/OSC/OSCST)
so OSC sequences (ESC ] ... BEL/ST) no longer leak payload bytes into the
reconstructed command string. Add terminal_test.go covering CSI, OSC (BEL and
ST terminated), backspace, multi-command, empty line, and two-char escape cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ENG-2014): always quote audit msg so SigNoz regex matches

logrus only quotes msg when it contains spaces. Changing from "audit"
to "audit event" ensures the msg field is always emitted as
msg="audit event", which matches the SigNoz log parser regex.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ENG-2014): always quote audit msg so SigNoz regex matches

logrus only quotes msg when it contains spaces. Changing from "audit"
to "audit event" ensures the msg field is always emitted as
msg="audit event", which matches the SigNoz log parser regex.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Add PoolConfig with VMSize and ImageResourceID

feat: initial AI Engineering Platform

LangGraph-based autonomous engineering agent that receives feature
requests in natural language and implements them end-to-end:
  analyze codebase → design → implement → test → commit & push

Components:
- agent/: LangGraph graph with 7 nodes (setup, analyze, design,
  implement, test, fix, commit_push) + streaming entry point
- mcp_servers/: FastMCP servers for filesystem, bash, git, pytest
- web/: FastAPI + WebSocket chat UI with real-time phase streaming
- config/: central settings loading from config/.env
- main.py: entry point (--check, --agent CLI, web UI)

LiteLLM Proxy integration: claude-sonnet-4-6 (main),
claude-haiku-4-5 (fast phases), with 6 models available.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Continuous voice dictation for Smart Pantry with add/remove support

Rebuild voice input from single-shot to continuous dictation mode:
- SpeechRecognition with continuous=true, interimResults=true
- Live transcript modal with pulse animation and real-time text display
- Auto-restart on browser stop for uninterrupted recording
- Parser detects "remove/toss/finished/used up" keywords for removals

- Reframe all three normative docs: ACT subsumes TFT (not "extends")
- Add "Known Fragilities" sections to README and CLAUDE.md: object model
  type error (G_t point vs DAG), edge identifiability gap, missing
  commitment/resource/temporal structure, DAG acyclicity assumption scope,
  cognitive cost of Σ_t, WEIGHTED rule should be dropped
- Expand PLANS Phase 1.1: drop WEIGHTED, add identifiability section,
  write Σ_t update dynamics formally
- Expand PLANS Phase 1.3: type inconsistency diagnosis, commitment/
  resource/temporal gaps, survival-needs-Σ_t insight
- Add Appendix F correlation caveat to Phase 2.4
- Reframe TST as temporal optimization target (not theorem)
- Reference agentic-tft docs 10-14 for logozoetic domain instantiation
- Add open questions 9-11 (cognitive cost, acyclicity scope, δ_feasibility)
- Add Hafez bridge passive-vs-active caveat
- Fix sim2 print (1.5 not 2.0), spec distribution (half-normal not
  exponential), spec R-sweep prediction (1.5 not 2.0)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Claude invocation by using inference profile instead of model ID

Issue: ValidationException when invoking Claude Sonnet 4.6:
"Invocation of model ID anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6 with on-demand
throughput isn't supported. Retry your request with the ID or ARN
of an inference profile that contains this model."

Root cause: Claude 4.6 models require using inference profiles,
not direct model IDs.

Solution: Changed default from direct model ID to inference profile:
- Old: anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6 (direct model ID - not supported)
- New: us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6 (US inference profile - works!)

Available ACTIVE inference profiles (confirmed via aws bedrock):
- us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6 (US cross-region) ⭐ NEW DEFAULT
- global.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6 (global cross-region)
- us.anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v1:0 (fast/cheap)
- us.anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620-v1:0 (older but stable)

Users can now use --claude-model-id with any inference profile:
  --claude-model-id global.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6
  --claude-model-id us.anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v1:0

Updated help text to show correct inference profile format.

  config, integrations, notifications, AI, data, and automations.
  Mode-aware (dispatch tenants not flagged for missing calendar, etc.)
  Returns actionable issues with severity levels and fix suggestions.

- automation-executor: processes automation_rules when business events
  occur. Supports send_sms, send_webhook, create_event, append_row.
  Logs every run and step to automation_runs/automation_run_steps.
  Called by other edge functions when events happen.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(WINT-9106): address 13 CodeRabbit review issues from PR #464 (#512)

Fixes all critical, major, minor, and nitpick issues raised by CodeRabbit:

- id=1 (critical): Checkpoint now captures post-node state snapshot instead
  of pre-execution stateSnapshot in withCheckpointer()
- id=2 (major): Add kb_get_roadmap to ToolNameSchema and ACCESS_MATRIX in
  access-control.ts (was returning FORBIDDEN for all callers)
- id=3 (major): Replace WithCheckpointerOptions and CheckpointedNodeResult
  TypeScript interfaces with Zod schemas (WithCheckpointerOptionsSchema,
  CheckpointedNodeResultSchema) per Zod-first requirement
- id=4 (major): Replace SELECT-then-INSERT/UPDATE in kb_create_story with
  atomic INSERT ON CONFLICT(story_id) DO NOTHING, eliminating race condition
- id=5 (major): Clear stale blockedReason/blockedByStory metadata when
  blocked is set to false in kb_create_story update path
- id=6 (major): Replace z.any() for acceptance_criteria with recursive
  JSONValueSchema in KbUpdateStoryInputSchema and KbCreateStoryInputSchema
- id=7 (minor): Update WORKABLE_STATUSES to use underscore-style DB status
  values (ready_to_work) in generateStoriesIndex.ts
- id=8 (minor): Constrain phase parameter in withCheckpointer() to Phase
  type from PHASE_TO_CHECKPOINT_MAP with runtime validation
- id=9 (minor): Surface actual checkpoint reached_at timestamp in
  resume-graph CLI instead of new Date().toISOString()
- id=10 (nitpick): Replace .strict() with .passthrough() on StoryMetadataSchema
  to avoid hard failures on harmless extra JSONB keys
- id=11 (nitpick): Fix ED-3 test to actually pass empty strings for
  story_dir and story_file
- id=12 (nitpick): Replace console.warn with logger.error and throw Error
  in beforeAll; remove conditional early returns from HP-3, HP-5, AC-5
- id=13 (nitpick): Add 'text' language specifier to fenced code block in
  VERIFICATION.md

Co-authored-by: Claude <claude@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: monorepo
repository.description: a monorepo architecture

commit.message:
chore(mobile-expo): CI lint, typecheck, and build for Expo app (#318)

* chore(mobile-expo): add CI lint, typecheck, and build for Expo app

Add @forge/expo to the affected-package detection in CI, exclude it
from the monorepo-wide lint job, and add a dedicated lint-expo job
that runs lint (--max-warnings=0), typecheck, and build when Expo
code changes. Mirrors the existing lint-ios and lint-android pattern.

Resolves #96

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(mobile-expo): swap no-op build script for expo export

Replace the echo placeholder with `npx expo export --platform all`
so CI validates the full Metro JS bundle for iOS and Android,
catching broken imports and missing assets that typecheck alone
cannot detect.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): exclude @forge/expo from monorepo-wide build step

The build-and-test job runs `pnpm build` across all packages, which
now triggers `expo export --platform all` for @forge/expo. This fails
because web dependencies (react-dom, react-native-web) are not
installed. Exclude @forge/expo (and other mobile/infra packages) from
the monorepo-wide build, matching the lint exclusion pattern. The
dedicated lint-expo job handles Expo builds independently.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* revert: remove package.json build change and build exclusion

Revert the expo export build swap and build-and-test exclusion.
The package.json change is out of scope for this CI-only issue.
The build step remains a no-op echo, so no exclusion is needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Initial rag-data-firewall MVP: OPA-style policy engine for secure RAG

Firewall-ordered rules evaluate retrieval requests against chunk
sensitivity labels, trust levels, source origin, requester type,
and session trust. Actions: allow, deny, redact, require-approval.
Every decision includes full evidence chain.

Scanners: prompt injection (14 patterns), PII detection (6 types),
suspicious code patterns, composite risk scoring.

CLI: serve, ingest, scan, query, list
Daemon API on :8500 with deny-by-default policy
25 tests passing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: rag-data-firewall
repository.description: OPA-style policy engine for secure RAG — sensitivity labels, retrieval rules, chunk trust levels, prompt injection defense, and full decision evidence

commit.message:
fix: normalize success metrics and bypass smoke test gate for SD-LEARN SDs

Addresses PAT-AUTO-41b51e4d and PAT-AUTO-77fe50e3:
- SUCCESS_METRICS_ACHIEVEMENT gate now normalizes plain string metrics to
  {name, target, actual} objects before scoring (prevents 0/100 failures)
- SMOKE_TEST_EVIDENCE gate bypasses SDs with SD-LEARN- prefix (prevents
  false positives on process-improvement SDs that reference pipeline keywords)
- Child SD template includes actual:null in all inherited success metrics

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
v2.7.0: Graceful shutdown, Firestore rules fix, UI improvements

- Add graceful shutdown: sync all data to Firebase on app exit
  (patients, notes, emergency contacts, appointments, settings)
- Fix Firestore rules: allow doctor to update patient fields
  (was blocking encrypted name/phone push)
- Fix online booking toggle: checked=enabled(green), unchecked=disabled(red)
- Change patient detail labels: "פגישות שנקבעו", "התקיימו"
- Bump installer to v2.7.0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use SSR-aware Supabase client in RealtimeComments to prevent TDZ crash

RealtimeComments was importing a standalone supabase instance from lib/supabase,
creating a duplicate GoTrueClient that conflicted with AuthProvider's client.
This caused "Cannot access 'B' before initialization" in production builds.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
